'''Belo Tusus''' was the ruler of [[Orto/Legends|Orto]] in the late stages of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. Originally the neutral [[Ortolan government]]'s [[Financial Minister]], Tusus aligned himself with the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], which was embroiled in the Clone Wars against the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] at the [[Time/Legends|time]]. Tusus conspired to overthrow the government, and did so, installing himself at the head of his [[Tusus government|new government]] and pushing Orto into the pan-[[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] conflict. Consolidating his power in the [[Capital of Orto|capital city]], Tusus ruled from a [[Orto palace|palace]], and locked down his [[Planet/Legends|planet's]] [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceports]] as Orto underwent Confederate occupation. His populace could do little but cower as [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] swept through the [[Capital/Legends|capital]].

Tusus's reign was soon disrupted in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]] by a series of [[Saboteur/Legends|sabotages]] and attacks. He grew paranoid as the undermining of his government's movements continued, and he began to suspect his own staff, and his Confederate allies. He soon lost trust in the Confederacy, as [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Nem Bees]], working undercover to investigate Tusus, had collaborated in secret with Tusus's own [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Belo Tusus's servant|servant]] to sabotage the new government. The [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] Knight subsequently called in the forces of the Galactic Republic, and moved against Tusus's rule. Betrayed and fearing for his own life, Tusus fled Orto, causing Bees and the Republic to fight a protracted campaign in the last days of the Clone Wars.

==Biography==
===Usurper===
Belo Tusus was the [[Financial Minister]] of [[Orto/Legends|Orto]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. Although the [[Ortolan government]] had remained neutral throughout the war, Tusus favored the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], rather than the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. As a wave of secession from the Republic swept through the outer regions of the [[Rimma Trade Route/Legends|Rimma Trade Route]], Tusus conspired to launch a ''coup d'etat'' against the current Ortolan government, seize control of Orto, and align his [[Planet/Legends|world]] with the Confederacy of Independent Systems. The presence of the conspiracy was ascertained by [[Republic Intelligence/Legends|Republic Intelligence]], but the fact that Tusus was the dissident element remained a secret.<ref name="db" />

Soon enough, Tusus launched his bid for power, publicly announcing his support of the Confederacy, before overthrowing the current government and installing himself as the planetary leader. He had all of Orto's [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceports]] locked down, as his allies from the Confederacy arrived. The new arrivals moved into the [[Capital of Orto|capital city]], their [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] marching down the streets, and Tusus was assigned Confederate [[Neimoidian/Legends|Neimoidian]] attachés. The new ruler of Orto then began cementing his control of the planet, working to settle in [[Tusus government|his government]] as he reigned from the [[Capital/Legends|capital's]] [[Orto palace|palace]].<ref name="db" />

===Usurped===
[[File:Nembees.jpg|thumb|right|180px|Jedi Knight Nem Bees, the thorn in Belo Tusus's side]]
By [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], Tusus's rule soon met resistance, in the form of unexplained acts of sabotage, of which there were several. Seemingly for no reason, [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droid]] detachments refused to deploy, and the guidance systems of [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttles]] had been clandestinely severed. As the effective strikes against his government continued, Tusus grew more and more paranoid, suspecting both his own staff of duplicity and his Confederate attachés. In fact, the traitor to Tusus's rule was his own [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Belo Tusus's servant|servant]], who had been leaking information to the [[Ortolan/Legends|Ortolan]] [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Nem Bees]]. Bees had in fact been operating on Orto prior to Tusus's ''coup'', dispatched by the Republic to investigate the Financial Minister's movements. Bees had avoided detection by Tusus's government by masquerading as the [[Merchant/Legends|trader]] [[Ydde]],<ref name="db" /> who had in fact died some [[Time/Legends|years]] prior.<ref name="ptr">[[Star Wars (1998) 2|''Star Wars'' (1998) 2]]</ref>

In addition to betraying sensitive details of Tusus's government to Bees, the usurper's servant also provided the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] Knight with contact to a [[HoloNet transceiver/Legends|HoloNet transceiver]]. This allowed the undercover Ortolan to contact the Republic and call in a force of [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]], while he infiltrated the palace and knocked down a vital [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|shield generator]]. Tusus did not trust the Confederacy to provide him with protection, and as he feared for his life, he fled Orto. The Republic forces, led by Bees, waged a protracted campaign against Tusus's entrenched Confederate forces, during which the Jedi Knight was [[Order 66/Legends|killed by his own men]].<ref name="db" />

==Personality and traits==
When plotting to overthrow the Ortolan government and install himself as the planet's new leader, Tusus was able to operate in such a way that the Republic was unaware that he was the culprit, although they were aware of a plot. He also kept his allegiance to the Confederacy quiet until his public announcement. Tusus forced his neutral planet into pan-galactic war on the side of the Confederacy, and allowed [[Troop/Legends|troops]] to descend on his world,<ref name="db" /> frightening his populace.<ref name="cw22">{{CW|22}}</ref> Once in power, Tusus moved quickly, solidifying his rule and securing the planet. However, he soon grew overly paranoid, suspecting his own government and the Confederacy itself of betrayal. His paranoia blinded him to the true traitor—his Twi'lek servant. When his position of power was threatened by Republican invasion, Tusus abandoned his world, as he had no trust in his Confederate allies and felt that his life was in peril.<ref name="db" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|Belo Tusus, financial minister of Orto, and his Twi'lek servant (edited from the original "slave", interestingly); an odd combination, to be sure, but not one unheard of. For any who read Republic #79, we see a rich Ortolan and his Twi'lek slave/servant in a marketplace on Toola. The Ortolan proceeds to get his money stolen by a Jedi, an act which the Twi'lek sees, finds amusing, and says nothing about. That's my man Belo, although the reference to Toola was cut.|Kyle Jewhurst|<ref name="blog">{{Blog|lordhydronium|1|Tales of a Jedi: The Creation of Nem Bees}}</ref>}}
[[File:BeloTusus.jpg|thumb|left|190px|The Ortolan from the ''Republic'' line that Jewhurst intended to be Tusus]]
The character of Belo Tusus was created by [[Kyle Jewhurst]] through the defunct [[StarWars.com]] [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]] feature "[[What's The Story?]]" Tusus is mentioned in Jewhurst's [[Databank (original)|Databank]] entry for the character of Nem Bees, who appeared briefly in the episode "[[Chapter 22 (Star Wars: Clone Wars)|Chapter 22]]" of the television series ''[[Star Wars: Clone Wars]]''. Jewhurst used the name of another Ortolan, [[Max Rebo/Legends|Max Rebo]], as the basis for Bees's and Tusus's names—simple syllables, two maximum. According to Jewhurst, the name "Belo Tusus" took approximately thirty seconds of fiddling around with letters to come up with.<ref name="blog" />

The character of Tusus himself was meant to be a rich Ortolan seen briefly in [[Republic 79|''Republic'' 79]]. The character is shown on [[Toola/Legends|Toola]] in a crowded marketplace, and is accompanied by a Twi'lek slave. A [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]], [[Kai Hudorra]], who is in hiding, steals the Ortolan's purse—the slave notices this, and is amused. The Ortolan, noticing that he has been robbed, strikes his aide for not alerting him to the theft.<ref name="Republic 79">[[Republic 79|''Republic'' 79]]</ref> In Jewhurst's original entry, a reference was made in which Tusus flees to Toola, although this was cut by [[Lucas Online]] staff before the Databank entry was published. Additionally, while Jewhurst intended for Tusus to be an Ortolan,<ref name="blog" /> he is not explicitly identified as such in the final Databank entry.<ref name="db" />
